headline: SUHAKAM's 15 recommendations ignored by government since 2020
context: The Human Rights Commission of Malaysia has had 15 formal recommendations to Parliament ignored by the executive since 2020, including on deaths in custody and indigenous land rights.

cards.big: 15 SUHAKAM recommendations ignored in 6 years — human rights body toothless
cards.sub: Government treats national human rights commission as advisory decoration

cards.big: SUHAKAM Act 1999 Section 4 gives only advisory, not binding, powers
cards.sub: Parliament must act on recommendations but no penalty exists for inaction

cards.big: Deaths in custody averaged 42 per year from 2020-2025 — SUHAKAM inquiry demanded reforms
cards.sub: 252 custodial deaths in six years with no systemic reform implemented

cards.big: Paris Principles require national human rights bodies to have effective mandates
cards.sub: SUHAKAM risks accreditation downgrade from A to B status by GANHRI

cards.big: A human rights commission without enforcement power is a suggestion box with official letterhead
Should SUHAKAM recommendations become automatically binding if Parliament doesn't respond in 12 months?
